Paste into TextEdit separately to confirm the fixture looks realistic with formatting preserved. ### html-parsing (₢A9AAD) [rough] ## Character Algorithmic — transform semi-structured HTML into a representation the detection engine can consume. ## Deliverables - `apcd/src/apcrp_parse.rs` — Parse Epic HTML using `scraper`. Extract: - Labeled fields: identify `Label: value` patterns, emit (label, value) pairs - Section boundaries: detect Epic section headers (Chief Complaint, HPI, PMH, Medications, Vitals, Labs, A/P, Signature) - Freetext runs: narrative text between labels/sections - Preserve source position information so findings can map back to the original text for rendering - Define data structures for parsed output: a sequence of typed spans (LabeledField, SectionHeader, Narrative, etc.) each carrying their text content and source offset - Plain text extraction path: strip all HTML, preserve line breaks and section structure ## Verification Feed the synthetic fixture through the parser. Print structured output showing detected labels, sections, and narrative spans with positions. All labeled PHI fields (Patient, DOB, MRN, Attending, Facility, etc.) correctly identified as LabeledField spans. ### dictionaries-detection-engine (₢A9AAE) [rough] ## Character The intellectual core. Two sub-problems: data acquisition (curating small dictionaries) and algorithm implementation (three-tier detection with merge). ## Deliverables **Dictionaries:** - `dictionaries/surnames.txt` — top 1,000 US surnames from Census 2010 data (public domain) - `dictionaries/firstnames.txt` — top 1,000 US first names from SSA baby names (public domain) - `dictionaries/cities.txt` — US state capitals + top 100 cities by population (public domain) - `dictionaries/medical_whitelist.txt` — ~500 curated medical terms: common medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy terms, medical abbreviations - `dictionaries/english_whitelist.txt` — top 5,000 common English words **`apcd/src/apcrd_dictionaries.rs`:** - Load dictionary files at startup - Build `aho-corasick` automaton from combined blacklist (surnames + firstnames + cities) - Build whitelist hash sets from medical + English terms - Expose lookup API: is_blacklisted, is_whitelisted, classify_word **`apcd/src/apcrm_match.rs`:** - Tier 1 regex patterns: SSN, phone, email, dates (multiple formats), street addresses, zip codes, MRN/account/encounter following labels - Tier 2 label-anchored extraction: words following known Epic labels → RED regardless of dictionary - Tier 3 dictionary scan: aho-corasick pass, whitelist filter, collision → YELLOW **`apcd/src/apcre_engine.rs`:** - Orchestrate: take parsed spans from `apcrp`, run all three tiers, merge by highest severity - Output: classified token stream — each token carries its text, category (RED/YELLOW/PASS), PHI type label (NAME, DATE, MRN, PHONE, etc.), and source position - DOB→age transform: when a date is labeled DOB, compute age from current date ## Verification Feed synthetic fixture through full pipeline (parse → detect → classify). Print findings list showing every detected PHI item with tier, severity, category label, and matched text. All names, dates, identifiers, addresses, phones in the fixture should be caught. Medical terms, medications, lab values, vitals should pass clean. ### frontend-triage-view (₢A9AAF) [rough] ## Character UX implementation. HTML/CSS/JS frontend work in the Tauri webview, wired to Rust backend results via Tauri IPC. ## Deliverables - `apcd/ui/index.html` updated: two-pane layout — document preview (top/left) and findings panel (bottom/right) - `apcd/ui/style.css` updated: RED (`background: #ff000030; border: 2px solid red`), YELLOW (`background: #ffff0030; border: 2px solid orange`), GREY (`color: #999`) inline highlight styles - `apcd/ui/app.js` updated: receive classified token stream from Rust backend via Tauri `invoke`, render document preview with inline highlights, populate findings panel with detection entries and ELIDE/PASS toggle dropdowns - Tauri IPC commands: `consume_clipboard` (triggered on window focus), returns classified token stream as JSON - Findings panel entries show: matched text, PHI category label, [ELIDE ▼] / [PASS ▼] toggle - Toggle changes update the document preview in real time (ELIDE → red/yellow box, PASS → grey text) - Default state: RED items locked to ELIDE, YELLOW items default ELIDE but toggleable, PASS items shown as grey ## Verification Load fixture → focus app → see the synthetic Epic note rendered with names in red boxes, questionable words in yellow boxes, medical terms and clinical content in grey. Toggle a yellow finding to PASS — preview updates. Visual confirmation against the synthetic fixture that all PHI is highlighted and all clinical content passes clean. ### anonymized-output (₢A9AAG) [rough] ## Character Pipeline completion. Wire the "Copy Anonymized" button to produce clean text from the triage state. ## Deliverables - `apcre_engine.rs` updated: generate anonymized plain text from classified token stream respecting current ELIDE/PASS state - Category-specific placeholders: `[NAME]`, `[DATE]`, `[MRN]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`, `[EMAIL]`, `[SSN]`, `[FACILITY]`, `[PROVIDER]`, `[ACCOUNT]`, `[ENCOUNTER_ID]` - DOB→age transform: dates labeled as DOB emit `Age: NN` instead of `[DATE]` - PASS items: emit original text unchanged - ELIDE items: emit placeholder for their PHI category - Preserve line breaks and section structure from parsed output — plain text should be readable, not a wall - Tauri IPC command: `copy_anonymized` — takes current toggle state from frontend, generates clean text, writes to clipboard via `arboard::set_text()` - Frontend: "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" button at bottom of window, calls `copy_anonymized` ## Verification Load fixture → triage view appears → toggle a few yellow items to PASS → click "Copy Anonymized" → paste into a text editor. Verify: all RED items replaced with category placeholders, DOB replaced with computed age, toggled PASS items preserved as original text, section structure readable, no PHI leaks. Text should be suitable for pasting into Open Evidence. ### self-update-deploy (₢A9AAH) [rough] ## Character Infrastructure and platform integration. Two independent pieces: self-update mechanism in the app, and the deploy tabtarget that ships to Ann's machine. ## Deliverables **Self-update (`apcd/src/apcru_update.rs`):** - Watch `/Users/Shared/apcua/` using `notify` crate (FSEvents on macOS) - On new `.app` bundle detected: copy over current app bundle, spawn `open -n` on new path, exit current process - No confirmation dialog — fully automatic - Graceful handling: if watch directory doesn't exist, create it silently and keep watching - Run watcher on a background thread, independent of main Tauri event loop **Deploy tabtarget (`tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh`):** - `cargo tauri build` (release) - `scp` the `.app` bundle to `anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/` - Report success with bundle size and path **Integration:** - Wire `apcru_update` into `apcap_main.rs` — start watcher on app launch - Update `apck-claude-context.md` with deploy workflow documentation ## Verification 1. Build and run app locally. Confirm watcher starts (log message). 2. Copy a dummy `.app` to `/Users/Shared/apcua/` — app detects it (may not self-replace locally since paths differ, but detection fires). 3. `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` completes: bundle appears on Ann's machine at `/Users/Shared/apcua/`. 4. End-to-end: Ann launches app, we deploy an update, app auto-restarts with new version. File-touch bitmap (x = pace commit touched file): 1 A write-specifications A x APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md, APCS-Specification.md, apck-claude-context.md Commit swim lanes (x = commit affiliated with pace): 1 A write-specifications 123456789abcdefg ···········xxxx· A 4c

Visual confirmation against the synthetic fixture that all PHI is highlighted and all clinical content passes clean. ### anonymized-output (₢A9AAG) [rough] ## Character Pipeline completion. Wire the "Copy Anonymized" button to produce clean text from the triage state. ## Deliverables - `apcre_engine.rs` updated: generate anonymized plain text from classified token stream respecting current ELIDE/PASS state - Category-specific placeholders: `[NAME]`, `[DATE]`, `[MRN]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`, `[EMAIL]`, `[SSN]`, `[FACILITY]`, `[PROVIDER]`, `[ACCOUNT]`, `[ENCOUNTER_ID]` - DOB→age transform: dates labeled as DOB emit `Age: NN` instead of `[DATE]` - PASS items: emit original text unchanged - ELIDE items: emit placeholder for their PHI category - Preserve line breaks and section structure from parsed output — plain text should be readable, not a wall - Tauri IPC command: `copy_anonymized` — takes current toggle state from frontend, generates clean text, writes to clipboard via `arboard::set_text()` - Frontend: "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" button at bottom of window, calls `copy_anonymized` ## Verification Load fixture → triage view appears → toggle a few yellow items to PASS → click "Copy Anonymized" → paste into a text editor. Verify: all RED items replaced with category placeholders, DOB replaced with computed age, toggled PASS items preserved as original text, section structure readable, no PHI leaks. Text should be suitable for pasting into Open Evidence. ### self-update-deploy (₢A9AAH) [rough] ## Character Infrastructure and platform integration. Two independent pieces: self-update mechanism in the app, and the deploy tabtarget that ships to Ann's machine. ## Deliverables **Self-update (`apcd/src/apcru_update.rs`):** - Watch `/Users/Shared/apcua/` using `notify` crate (FSEvents on macOS) - On new `.app` bundle detected: copy over current app bundle, spawn `open -n` on new path, exit current process - No confirmation dialog — fully automatic - Graceful handling: if watch directory doesn't exist, create it silently and keep watching - Run watcher on a background thread, independent of main Tauri event loop **Deploy tabtarget (`tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh`):** - `cargo tauri build` (release) - `scp` the `.app` bundle to `anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/` - Report success with bundle size and path **Integration:** - Wire `apcru_update` into `apcap_main.rs` — start watcher on app launch - Update `apck-claude-context.md` with deploy workflow documentation ## Verification 1. Build and run app locally. Confirm watcher starts (log message). 2. Copy a dummy `.app` to `/Users/Shared/apcua/` — app detects it (may not self-replace locally since paths differ, but detection fires). 3. `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` completes: bundle appears on Ann's machine at `/Users/Shared/apcua/`. 4. End-to-end: Ann launches app, we deploy an update, app auto-restarts with new version. File-touch bitmap (x = pace commit touched file): 1 A write-specifications A x APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md, APCS-Specification.md, apck-claude-context.md Commit swim lanes (x = commit affiliated with pace): 1 A write-specifications 123456789abcdefg ···········xxxx· A 4c

content.content: 1 # jjezs_paddock ₣A9 2 3 ## Character 4 5 Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe. 6 7 ## Problem 8 9 Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage. 10 11 ## Solution 12 13 Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that: 14 1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption 15 2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections) 16 3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary) 17 4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass) 18 5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section) 19 6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+") 20 7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation 21 22 ## Key Design Decisions 23 24 - **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS 25 - **Case-insensitive**: All dictionary lookups normalize to lowercase; original case preserved in display 26 - **Per-word anchoring**: No multi-word merge — each word individually classified 27 - **Conservative bias**: False positive preferred over false negative 28 - **Provider/facility names**: Treated as PHI (re-identifying in combination with clinical details) 29 - **Clipboard always cleared** after consumption 30 - **Clinical content heuristic** gates processing; non-clinical clipboard shows diagnostic and clears triage 31 32 ## Authoritative Documents 33 34 | Document | Owns | Path | 35 |----------|------|------| 36 | Product Spec (APCS) | UX plan, workflow, deployment, HIPAA context | `Tools/apck/APCS-Specification.md` | 37 | Prototype Spec (APCPS) | Detection pipeline, tech stack, dictionaries, project structure, scope boundaries | `Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` | 38 | Claude Context | Build/run/deploy instructions, file mappings, prefix tree, tabtargets | `Tools/apck/apck-claude-context.md` | 39 | Rust Coding Guide (RCG) | Naming conventions, test patterns, disciplines | `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md` | 40 41 # jjezs_pace ₢A9AAA 42 43 ## Character 44 45 Synthesis and writing. Distill the design conversation into two complementary documents with different audiences and lifespans. 46 47 ## Deliverables 48 49 **APCS-Specification.md (Product Spec):** 50 - Full UX plan: focus-to-consume workflow, three-category triage (red/yellow/grey), findings panel with ELIDE/PASS toggles, "Copy Anonymized" button, DOB→age transform 51 - Self-update behavior description (user-facing: app updates itself silently) 52 - Deployment model (scp to shared directory, Ann launches independently) 53 - Target platforms (macOS first, Windows planned) 54 - Open Evidence context and HIPAA Safe Harbor motivation 55 - Pipeline/detection section: deliberately blank, marked with note pointing to APCPS as the living engineering document 56 - Redistribution considerations (public domain dictionaries, SNOMED/NLM deferred) 57 58 **APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md (Prototype Spec):** 59 - Full three-tier detection pipeline: regex → label-anchored → dictionary 60 - Tier 1 regex patterns enumerated (SSN, phone, date, email, address, zip, labeled identifiers) 61 - Tier 2 Epic label vocabulary (Patient:, Attending:, Facility:, etc.) 62 - Tier 3 dictionary strategy: blacklist/whitelist, collision resolution, curated-small sizing 63 - Anonymization strategy: category-specific placeholders, DOB→age transform, plain text output 64 - Tech stack with crate choices and rationale 65 - Project structure with full file tree and prefix tree 66 - Scope boundaries: no persistence, one patient, no configuration, no click-to-highlight 67 - NO UX plan (lives in APCS only) 68 69 ## Approach 70 71 Read the paddock thoroughly — it captures the full design conversation. Translate into spec prose. Ensure both documents cross-reference each other at their boundary. 72 73 # jjezs_pace ₢A9AAB 74 75 ## Character 76 77 Mechanical with minting attention. Create every file and directory, wire BUK dispatch, get first Tauri window on screen. 78 79 ## Deliverables 80 81 - `Tools/apck/` directory with full tree per paddock 82 - `apcw_workbench.sh` — routes `apcw-*` commands 83 - `apcz_zipper.sh` — enrolls colophons with BUK dispatch 84 - `apck-claude-context.md` — build, run, deploy instructions; references RCG as Rust coding guide 85 - `apcd/Cargo.toml` — two `[[bin]]` targets: `apcap` (Tauri app), `apcal` (fixture loader) 86 - `apcd/src/apcap_main.rs` — Tauri entry point, launches window with static "Hello from APCK" text 87 - `apcd/src/lib.rs` — module declarations (empty stubs for engine modules) 88 - `apcd/ui/index.html`, `style.css`, `app.js` — minimal Tauri frontend 89 - Tabtargets: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh`, `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh`, `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh`, `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` 90 - `test_fixtures/` directory (empty, ready for next pace) 91 - `apcd/dictionaries/` directory (empty, ready for later pace) 92 - Tauri config (`tauri.conf.json` or Cargo-based Tauri 2.x config) 93 94 ## Verification 95 96 `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` launches a Tauri window displaying static text. `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` produces a `.app` bundle. 97 98 # jjezs_pace ₢A9AAC 99 100 ## Character 101 102 Integration work — two independent pieces (fixture loader + clipboard consumption) that meet in the middle. 103 104 ## Deliverables 105 106 - `test_fixtures/epic_progress_note.html` — synthetic Epic "Copy All" HTML with realistic PHI across all 18 HIPAA categories. Include: demographics header with labeled fields, narrative HPI with embedded names/addresses/phones, vitals, labs, medications, assessment/plan with provider names, signature block. 107 - `apcd/src/apcal_main.rs` — fixture loader binary. Uses `arboard` to write HTML file contents to clipboard as HTML format. Takes fixture file path as argument. 108 - `apcap_main.rs` updated: on Tauri window focus, read clipboard HTML via `arboard::get_html()`, fall back to `get_text()`. Display raw clipboard content in the webview. 109 - `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` wired to run `apcal` with the default fixture. 110 111 ## Verification 112 113 Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh`, then focus the Tauri app — raw Epic HTML content appears in the window. Paste into TextEdit separately to confirm the fixture looks realistic with formatting preserved. 114 115 # jjezs_pace ₢A9AAD 116 117 ## Character 118 119 Algorithmic — transform semi-structured HTML into a representation the detection engine can consume. 120 121 ## Deliverables 122 123 - `apcd/src/apcrp_parse.rs` — Parse Epic HTML using `scraper`. Extract: 124 - Labeled fields: identify `Label: value` patterns, emit (label, value) pairs 125 - Section boundaries: detect Epic section headers (Chief Complaint, HPI, PMH, Medications, Vitals, Labs, A/P, Signature) 126 - Freetext runs: narrative text between labels/sections 127 - Preserve source position information so findings can map back to the original text for rendering 128 - Define data structures for parsed output: a sequence of typed spans (LabeledField, SectionHeader, Narrative, etc.) each carrying their text content and source offset 129 - Plain text extraction path: strip all HTML, preserve line breaks and section structure 130 131 ## Verification 132 133 Feed the synthetic fixture through the parser. Print structured output showing detected labels, sections, and narrative spans with positions. All labeled PHI fields (Patient, DOB, MRN, Attending, Facility, etc.) correctly identified as LabeledField spans. 134 135 # jjezs_pace ₢A9AAE 136 137 ## Character 138 139 The intellectual core. Two sub-problems: data acquisition (curating small dictionaries) and algorithm implementation (three-tier detection with merge). 140 141 ## Deliverables 142 143 **Dictionaries:** 144 - `dictionaries/surnames.txt` — top 1,000 US surnames from Census 2010 data (public domain) 145 - `dictionaries/firstnames.txt` — top 1,000 US first names from SSA baby names (public domain) 146 - `dictionaries/cities.txt` — US state capitals + top 100 cities by population (public domain) 147 - `dictionaries/medical_whitelist.txt` — ~500 curated medical terms: common medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy terms, medical abbreviations 148 - `dictionaries/english_whitelist.txt` — top 5,000 common English words 149 150 **`apcd/src/apcrd_dictionaries.rs`:** 151 - Load dictionary files at startup 152 - Build `aho-corasick` automaton from combined blacklist (surnames + firstnames + cities) 153 - Build whitelist hash sets from medical + English terms 154 - Expose lookup API: is_blacklisted, is_whitelisted, classify_word 155 156 **`apcd/src/apcrm_match.rs`:** 157 - Tier 1 regex patterns: SSN, phone, email, dates (multiple formats), street addresses, zip codes, MRN/account/encounter following labels 158 - Tier 2 label-anchored extraction: words following known Epic labels → RED regardless of dictionary 159 - Tier 3 dictionary scan: aho-corasick pass, whitelist filter, collision → YELLOW 160 161 **`apcd/src/apcre_engine.rs`:** 162 - Orchestrate: take parsed spans from `apcrp`, run all three tiers, merge by highest severity 163 - Output: classified token stream — each token carries its text, category (RED/YELLOW/PASS), PHI type label (NAME, DATE, MRN, PHONE, etc.), and source position 164 - DOB→age transform: when a date is labeled DOB, compute age from current date 165 166 ## Verification 167 168 Feed synthetic fixture through full pipeline (parse → detect → classify). Print findings list showing every detected PHI item with tier, severity, category label, and matched text. All names, dates, identifiers, addresses, phones in the fixture should be caught. Medical terms, medications, lab values, vitals should pass clean. 169 170 # jjezs_pace ₢A9AAF 171 172 ## Character 173 174 UX implementation. Toggle a yellow finding to PASS — preview updates. HTML/CSS/JS frontend work in the Tauri webview, wired to Rust backend results via Tauri IPC. ## Deliverables - `apcd/ui/index.html` updated: two-pane layout — document preview (top/left) and findings panel (bottom/right) - `apcd/ui/style.css` updated: RED (`background: #ff000030; border: 2px solid red`), YELLOW (`background: #ffff0030; border: 2px solid orange`), GREY (`color: #999`) inline highlight styles - `apcd/ui/app.js` updated: receive classified token stream from Rust backend via Tauri `invoke`, render document preview with inline highlights, populate findings panel with detection entries and ELIDE/PASS toggle dropdowns - Tauri IPC commands: `consume_clipboard` (triggered on window focus), returns classified token stream as JSON - Findings panel entries show: matched text, PHI category label, [ELIDE ▼] / [PASS ▼] toggle - Toggle changes update the document preview in real time (ELIDE → red/yellow box, PASS → grey text) - Default state: RED items locked to ELIDE, YELLOW items default ELIDE but toggleable, PASS items shown as grey ## Verification Load fixture → focus app → see the synthetic Epic note rendered with names in red boxes, questionable words in yellow boxes, medical terms and clinical content in grey. Toggle a yellow finding to PASS — preview updates. Visual confirmation against the synthetic fixture that all PHI is highlighted and all clinical content passes clean. # jjezs_pace ₢A9AAG ## Character Pipeline completion. Wire the "Copy Anonymized" button to produce clean text from the triage state. ## Deliverables - `apcre_engine.rs` updated: generate anonymized plain text from classified token stream respecting current ELIDE/PASS state - Category-specific placeholders: `[NAME]`, `[DATE]`, `[MRN]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`, `[EMAIL]`, `[SSN]`, `[FACILITY]`, `[PROVIDER]`, `[ACCOUNT]`, `[ENCOUNTER_ID]` - DOB→age transform: dates labeled as DOB emit `Age: NN` instead of `[DATE]` - PASS items: emit original text unchanged - ELIDE items: emit placeholder for their PHI category - Preserve line breaks and section structure from parsed output — plain text should be readable, not a wall - Tauri IPC command: `copy_anonymized` — takes current toggle state from frontend, generates clean text, writes to clipboard via `arboard::set_text()` - Frontend: "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" button at bottom of window, calls `copy_anonymized` ## Verification Load fixture → triage view appears → toggle a few yellow items to PASS → click "Copy Anonymized" → paste into a text editor. Verify: all RED items replaced with category placeholders, DOB replaced with computed age, toggled PASS items preserved as original text, section structure readable, no PHI leaks. Text should be suitable for pasting into Open Evidence. # jjezs_pace ₢A9AAH ## Character Infrastructure and platform integration. Two independent pieces: self-update mechanism in the app, and the deploy tabtarget that ships to Ann's machine. ## Deliverables **Self-update (`apcd/src/apcru_update.rs`):** - Watch `/Users/Shared/apcua/` using `notify` crate (FSEvents on macOS) - On new `.app` bundle detected: copy over current app bundle, spawn `open -n` on new path, exit current process - No confirmation dialog — fully automatic - Graceful handling: if watch directory doesn't exist, create it silently and keep watching - Run watcher on a background thread, independent of main Tauri event loop **Deploy tabtarget (`tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh`):** - `cargo tauri build` (release) - `scp` the `.app` bundle to `anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/` - Report success with bundle size and path **Integration:** - Wire `apcru_update` into `apcap_main.rs` — start watcher on app launch - Update `apck-claude-context.md` with deploy workflow documentation ## Verification 1. Build and run app locally. Confirm watcher starts (log message). 2. Copy a dummy `.app` to `/Users/Shared/apcua/` — app detects it (may not self-replace locally since paths differ, but detection fires). 3. `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` completes: bundle appears on Ann's machine at `/Users/Shared/apcua/`. 4. End-to-end: Ann launches app, we deploy an update, app auto-restarts with new version.

content.text:
Here's my pace-by-pace review against the scrubbed specs and paddock:

**₢A9AAA write-specifications** — This pace is essentially complete. The docket still references "Read the paddock thoroughly — it captures the full design conversation" which was true at writing time but the paddock is now slim. No repair needed — the docket is historical context for a pace that's about to be wrapped.

**₢A9AAB scaffold-hello-world** — Two issues:
- Missing `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` tabtarget (added to specs/context but not to this docket)
- Says "full tree per paddock" but paddock no longer has the tree — should reference APCPS
- Missing: test file stubs (`apcte_engine.rs`, `apctp_parse.rs`, `apctm_match.rs`, `apctd_dictionaries.rs`) in lib.rs declarations
- Missing: `apck-claude-context.md` is listed as deliverable but already exists (created during ₢A9AAA)

**₢A9AAC fixture-clipboard-read** — Two issues:
- Says "on Tauri window focus, read clipboard HTML" but doesn't mention the clinical content heuristic or clipboard clearing. At this pace we should at minimum read and display, but the heuristic/clearing behavior is now specified. Should note: implement basic focus→consume, defer heuristic gating to detection engine pace.
- Doesn't mention clipboard change detection (store last consumed content for comparison)

**₢A9AAD html-parsing** — Clean. No conflicts. The docket accurately describes what APCPS specifies for the parser. Should mention `apctp_parse.rs` unit tests as a deliverable.

**₢A9AAE dictionaries-detection-engine** — Several issues:
- No mention of case-insensitive normalization (now specified in APCPS)
- No mention of anchored vs matched vocabulary distinction
- No mention of per-word anchoring for Tier 2
- Tier 2 says "words following known Epic labels → RED regardless of dictionary" which is correct but should use "anchored" terminology
- DOB→age doesn't mention >89→"90+" 
- No mention of clinical content heuristic being implemented here (this is where it logically lives — the engine decides if content is clinical)
- No mention of `apcte_engine.rs`, `apctm_match.rs`, `apctd_dictionaries.rs` unit tests
- Tier 1 known ambiguities not mentioned

**₢A9AAF frontend-triage-view** — Two conflicts:
- Line 184: "RED items locked to ELIDE" — contradicts APCS. RED items are toggleable with friction (separate panel section)
- Doesn't describe the two-section findings panel (Questionable upper / Definite PHI lower)
- Doesn't mention initial state (instruction screen before first consumption)
- Doesn't mention clipboard change detection behavior on refocus

**₢A9AAG anonymized-output** — One issue:
- DOB→age doesn't mention >89→"90+"

**₢A9AAH self-update-deploy** — Clean. No conflicts with specs.

**Summary — paces needing reslate:**

| Pace | Severity | Issues |
|------|----------|--------|
| ₢A9AAB | Minor | Add test tabtarget, test stubs, reference APCPS not paddock, note context.md exists |
| ₢A9AAC | Minor | Note heuristic/clearing deferred, add change detection storage |
| ₢A9AAD | Minor | Add unit test deliverable |
| ₢A9AAE | Moderate | Case handling, anchored/matched vocabulary, per-word anchoring, >89, heuristic, unit tests, known ambiguities |
| ₢A9AAF | Moderate | Fix RED toggleable, two-section panel, initial state, change detection |
| ₢A9AAG | Minor | Add >89→"90+" |

₢A9AAA and ₢A9AAH are clean. Want me to reslate the six that need repairs? I can do a mass reslate.

message.content: do proceed.

input.content: # jjezs_reslate A9AAB ## Character Mechanical with minting attention. Create every file and directory, wire BUK dispatch, get first Tauri window on screen. ## Deliverables - `Tools/apck/` directory with full tree per APCPS project structure section - `apcw_workbench.sh` — routes `apcw-*` commands - `apcz_zipper.sh` — enrolls colophons with BUK dispatch - `apck-claude-context.md` — already exists from ₢A9AAA; review and update if needed - `apcd/Cargo.toml` — two `[[bin]]` targets: `apcap` (Tauri app), `apcal` (fixture loader) - `apcd/src/apcap_main.rs` — Tauri entry point, launches window with static "Ann's PHI Clipbuddy" title and initial instruction text - `apcd/src/lib.rs` — module declarations (empty stubs) and test module declarations per APCPS: - Source: `apcre_engine`, `apcrp_parse`, `apcrm_match`, `apcrd_dictionaries`, `apcru_update` - Tests: `#[cfg(test)]` for `apcte_engine`, `apctp_parse`, `apctm_match`, `apctd_dictionaries` - Empty stub files for all source and test modules - `apcd/ui/index.html`, `style.css`, `app.js` — minimal Tauri frontend showing initial state - Tabtargets: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh`, `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh`, `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh`, `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh`, `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` - `test_fixtures/` directory (empty, ready for next pace) - `apcd/dictionaries/` directory (empty, ready for later pace) - Tauri config (`tauri.conf.json` or Cargo-based Tauri 2.x config) ## Verification - `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` launches a Tauri window displaying "Ann's PHI Clipbuddy" with instruction text - `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` produces a `.app` bundle - `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` runs cargo test (passes trivially — no tests yet) # jjezs_reslate A9AAC ## Character Integration work — two independent pieces (fixture loader + clipboard consumption) that meet in the middle. ## Deliverables - `test_fixtures/epic_progress_note.html` — synthetic Epic "Copy All" HTML with realistic PHI across all 18 HIPAA categories. Include: demographics header with labeled fields, narrative HPI with embedded names/addresses/phones, vitals, labs, medications, assessment/plan with provider names, signature block. Must contain 2+ clinical labels to pass the clinical content heuristic. - `apcd/src/apcal_main.rs` — fixture loader binary. Uses `arboard` to write HTML file contents to clipboard as HTML format. Takes fixture file path as argument. - `apcap_main.rs` updated: on Tauri window focus, read clipboard HTML via `arboard::get_html()`, fall back to `get_text()`. Display raw clipboard content in the webview. - Store last consumed clipboard content for change detection — on refocus, compare byte-for-byte; if unchanged, preserve current display without reprocessing. - `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` wired to run `apcal` with the default fixture. Note: Clinical content heuristic and clipboard clearing are deferred to ₢A9AAE (detection engine), where the heuristic logic naturally lives. This pace implements raw consume-and-display. ## Verification Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh`, then focus the Tauri app — raw Epic HTML content appears in the window. Refocus away and back — display preserved, no reprocessing. Paste into TextEdit separately to confirm the fixture looks realistic with formatting preserved. # jjezs_reslate A9AAD ## Character Algorithmic — transform semi-structured HTML into a representation the detection engine can consume. ## Deliverables - `apcd/src/apcrp_parse.rs` — Parse Epic HTML using `scraper`. Extract: - Labeled fields: identify `Label: value` patterns, emit (label, value) pairs - Section boundaries: detect Epic section headers (Chief Complaint, HPI, PMH, Medications, Vitals, Labs, A/P, Signature) - Freetext runs: narrative text between labels/sections - Preserve source position information so findings can map back to the original text for rendering - Define data structures for parsed output: a sequence of typed spans (LabeledField, SectionHeader, Narrative, etc.) each carrying their text content and source offset - Plain text extraction path: strip all HTML, preserve line breaks and section structure - `apcd/src/apctp_parse.rs` — unit tests: feed synthetic fixture HTML through parser, assert correct extraction of labeled fields, section boundaries, and narrative spans ## Verification - Unit tests pass via `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` - Feed the synthetic fixture through the parser. Print structured output showing detected labels, sections, and narrative spans with positions. All labeled PHI fields (Patient, DOB, MRN, Attending, Facility, etc.) correctly identified as LabeledField spans. # jjezs_reslate A9AAE ## Character The intellectual core. Data acquisition, algorithm implementation, and clinical content gating. Requires attention to the anchored/matched vocabulary distinction and case normalization. ## Deliverables **Dictionaries:** - `dictionaries/surnames.txt` — top 1,000 US surnames from Census 2010 data (public domain) - `dictionaries/firstnames.txt` — top 1,000 US first names from SSA baby names (public domain) - `dictionaries/cities.txt` — US state capitals + top 100 cities by population (public domain) - `dictionaries/medical_whitelist.txt` — ~500 curated medical terms: common medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy terms, medical abbreviations - `dictionaries/english_whitelist.txt` — top 5,000 common English words **`apcd/src/apcrd_dictionaries.rs`:** - Load dictionary files at startup - Build `aho-corasick` automaton from lowercase blacklist entries (surnames + firstnames + cities) - Build whitelist hash sets from lowercase medical + English terms - Case-insensitive lookups: input tokens lowercased before matching, original case preserved for display - Expose lookup API: is_blacklisted, is_whitelisted, classify_word **`apcd/src/apcrm_match.rs`:** - Tier 1 regex patterns: SSN, phone, email, dates (multiple formats), street addresses, zip codes, MRN/account/encounter following labels. Known ambiguities accepted (zip matching 5-digit numbers, date matching version numbers) — conservative bias, clinician reviews. - Tier 2 label-anchored extraction: per-word anchoring — each word following a known Epic label is individually flagged RED. These are **anchored** items, independent of any dictionary. Anchored classification is final. - Tier 3 dictionary scan: aho-corasick pass with case-insensitive matching, whitelist filter, collision → YELLOW. These are **matched** items. **`apcd/src/apcre_engine.rs`:** - Orchestrate: take parsed spans from `apcrp`, run all three tiers, merge by highest severity (anchored RED > matched YELLOW > PASS) - Output: classified token stream — each token carries its text, classification (RED/YELLOW/PASS), mechanism (anchored/matched/regex), PHI type label (NAME, DATE, MRN, PHONE, etc.), and source position - DOB→age transform: when a date is labeled DOB, compute age from current date. Ages >89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor (45 CFR 164.514(b)(2)(i)(C)). - Clinical content heuristic: scan for 2+ known clinical labels (Patient:, DOB:, MRN:, etc.) to gate processing. Non-clinical content returns a diagnostic (content length, type, first ~100 chars) for debugging. Clipboard cleared after successful consumption. **Unit tests:** - `apctd_dictionaries.rs` — dictionary loading, case-insensitive lookup, blacklist/whitelist/collision classification - `apctm_match.rs` — regex patterns against known examples (SSN, phone, dates, addresses), label-anchored per-word extraction, anchored vs matched precedence - `apcte_engine.rs` — full pipeline with synthetic fixture: all PHI caught, all medical terms pass, DOB→age with >89 handling, clinical heuristic acceptance/rejection ## Verification - All unit tests pass via `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` - Feed synthetic fixture through full pipeline (parse → detect → classify). Print findings list showing every detected PHI item with tier (anchored/matched/regex), severity, category label, and matched text. All names, dates, identifiers, addresses, phones in the fixture caught. Medical terms, medications, lab values, vitals pass clean. # jjezs_reslate A9AAF ## Character UX implementation. HTML/CSS/JS frontend work in the Tauri webview, wired to Rust backend results via Tauri IPC. ## Deliverables - `apcd/ui/index.html` updated: three states: 1. **Initial state**: "Ann's PHI Clipbuddy" title + "Copy a patient note from Epic, then switch to this window" instruction 2. **Non-clinical clipboard**: diagnostic line (content length, type, first ~100 chars), returns to instruction state 3. **Triage view**: two-pane layout — document preview (top/left) and findings panel (bottom/right) - `apcd/ui/style.css` updated: RED (`background: #ff000030; border: 2px solid red`), YELLOW (`background: #ffff0030; border: 2px solid orange`), GREY (`color: #999`) inline highlight styles - `apcd/ui/app.js` updated: receive classified token stream from Rust backend via Tauri `invoke`, render document preview with inline highlights, populate findings panel - Tauri IPC commands: `consume_clipboard` (triggered on window focus), returns classified token stream as JSON or non-clinical diagnostic - Findings panel with **two sections**: - **Questionable Findings** (upper, YELLOW): dictionary matches and ambiguous detections. Default ELIDE, easily toggled to PASS. - **Definite PHI** (lower, RED): label-anchored and regex-matched identifiers. Default ELIDE, toggleable to PASS but separated to create deliberate friction. - Each entry shows: matched text, PHI category label, [ELIDE ▼] / [PASS ▼] toggle - Toggle changes update the document preview in real time - Clipboard change detection on refocus: unchanged clipboard preserves triage state ## Verification Load fixture → focus app → see the synthetic Epic note rendered with names in red boxes, questionable words in yellow boxes, medical terms and clinical content in grey. YELLOW findings in upper panel section, RED in lower. Toggle a yellow finding to PASS — preview updates. Toggle a red finding to PASS — requires scrolling to lower section. Refocus without clipboard change — triage preserved. Visual confirmation against the synthetic fixture that all PHI is highlighted and all clinical content passes clean. # jjezs_reslate A9AAG ## Character Pipeline completion. Wire the "Copy Anonymized" button to produce clean text from the triage state. ## Deliverables - `apcre_engine.rs` updated: generate anonymized plain text from classified token stream respecting current ELIDE/PASS state - Category-specific placeholders: `[NAME]`, `[DATE]`, `[MRN]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`, `[EMAIL]`, `[SSN]`, `[FACILITY]`, `[PROVIDER]`, `[ACCOUNT]`, `[ENCOUNTER_ID]` - DOB→age transform: dates labeled as DOB emit `Age: NN` instead of `[DATE]`. Ages >89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor. - PASS items (including RED items toggled to PASS by clinician): emit original text unchanged - ELIDE items: emit placeholder for their PHI category - Preserve line breaks and section structure from parsed output — plain text should be readable, not a wall - Tauri IPC command: `copy_anonymized` — takes current toggle state from frontend, generates clean text, writes to clipboard via `arboard::set_text()` - Frontend: "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" button at bottom of window, calls `copy_anonymized` ## Verification Load fixture → triage view appears → toggle a few yellow items to PASS → toggle one red item to PASS → click "Copy Anonymized" → paste into a text editor. Verify: all ELIDE items replaced with category placeholders, DOB replaced with computed age (90+ for >89), toggled PASS items preserved as original text, section structure readable, no PHI leaks. Text should be suitable for pasting into Open Evidence.
